items.final_wording: Supports erectile-function onset, duration, rigidity, morning erections, desire, arousal, ejaculation, orgasm, relationship, treatment and symptom-impact documentation.

source.title: EAU Guidelines on Sexual and Reproductive Health — Management of Erectile Dysfunction
source.population: Adult men presenting with erectile dysfunction or related sexual-function concerns.
source.setting: Urology, sexual medicine and relevant endocrine assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected medical, sexual, psychosocial, cardiovascular-risk, focused examination and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation. It does not diagnose the cause, recommend treatment or define UAE referral pathways.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports erectile-function onset, duration, rigidity, morning erections, desire, arousal, ejaculation, orgasm, relationship, treatment and symptom-impact documentation.

items.rationale: The exact EAU history section supports onset, interval change, severity or impact, associated sexual symptoms, relevant negatives and patient goals without inferring erectile-dysfunction cause.

items.final_wording: Supports focused genitourinary, endocrine, vascular and neurological examination, blood pressure, heart rate, BMI or waist, glucose, HbA1c, lipids, morning testosterone and selected hormone context.

source.title: EAU Guidelines on Sexual and Reproductive Health — Management of Erectile Dysfunction
source.population: Adult men presenting with erectile dysfunction or related sexual-function concerns.
source.setting: Urology, sexual medicine and relevant endocrine assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected medical, sexual, psychosocial, cardiovascular-risk, focused examination and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation. It does not diagnose the cause, recommend treatment or define UAE referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5.4.2–5.4.3 — physical examination and laboratory testing

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports focused genitourinary, endocrine, vascular and neurological examination, blood pressure, heart rate, BMI or waist, glucose, HbA1c, lipids, morning testosterone and selected hormone context.

items.rationale: The exact EAU examination and laboratory section supports measured vital signs, weight or BMI, focused endocrine assessment, laboratory review and previous specialist correspondence only when performed or available.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-documented cardiovascular, mood, anxiety, life-stressor, relationship and psychosexual context without calculating risk or assigning cause.

source.title: EAU Guidelines on Sexual and Reproductive Health — Management of Erectile Dysfunction
source.population: Adult men presenting with erectile dysfunction or related sexual-function concerns.
source.setting: Urology, sexual medicine and relevant endocrine assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected medical, sexual, psychosocial, cardiovascular-risk, focused examination and clinician-reviewed laboratory documentation. It does not diagnose the cause, recommend treatment or define UAE referral pathways.

exact_location.heading: Sections 5.4.4 and 5.5.5 — cardiovascular and psychosocial assessment

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-documented cardiovascular, mood, anxiety, life-stressor, relationship and psychosexual context without calculating risk or assigning cause.

items.rationale: The exact cardiovascular and psychosocial section supports clinician concern, clinician-entered plan, discussed safety net, arranged follow-up and patient questions only when documented; it does not calculate cardiovascular risk or recommend treatment.
